titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD 4.3.Sacr.2 (previously, Misc. Ch. 716)

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=65 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.824381+00 | translation moa=66 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-02 13:02:56.812419+00

p.cl: Omnibus sancte matris ecclesie filiis Johannes de Vallibus salutem.

Sciant presentes ⁊ futuri . me con
cessisse ⁊ hac carta mea confirmasse deo ⁊ sancte Marie ⁊ sancto Cuthberto . ⁊ monachis de
Dunelmo . terram illam in territorio de Golin’ . quam Willelmus de Vallibus pater meus eis dedit
in puram ⁊ perpetuam elemosinam. Et uolo . ut Lucas filius Osmund’ . ⁊ heredes sui terram illam te
neant de eisdem monachis per easdem diuisas per quas idem Osmund . terram illam tenuit eo die quo
ipse fuit uiuus ⁊ mortuus . ⁊ cum omnibus aisiamentis ⁊ iustis pertinentiis suis . libere quiete plenarie
⁊ honorifice sicut carta dicti Willelmi patris mei dictis monachis . ⁊ Osmund . ⁊ heredibus eius inde facta
testatur . reddendo inde annuatim dictis monachis septem Solidos . ad festum sancti Michaelis .

Hiis testibus .
Roberto de Vallibus de Ellbotl’ . Willelmo de Munfichet’ . Walter de Lindes’ . Waltero de Fentun’ . Regi
naldo de Hotot’
To all the sons of Holy Mother Church John de Vaux sends greeting.

Let present and future know that I have granted and by this my charter made firm to God and to Saint Mary and to Saint Cuthbert, and to the monks of Durham, that land in the territory of Gullane, which William de Vaux my father gave to them in pure and perpetual alms. And I will, that Luke the son of Osmund and his own heirs should hold that land of the same monks by the same boundaries by which the same Osmund held that land on that day in which he was alive and dead, and with all its easements and just pertinents, freely, quietly, fully, and honourably, just as the charter of the said William my father, made thereupon for the said monks, and Osmund and his heirs, bears witness, rendering thereupon annually to the said monks seven shillings at the feast of Saint Michael.
With these witnesses : Robert de Vaux of Elbottle, William of Munfichet, Walter of Lindsay, Walter of Fenton, Reginald of Hoton.
